title: Add deterministic live OpenAI synthesis evaluator artifact
problem: Live OpenAI synthesis canary outputs currently require manual inspection of synthesis_packet_run JSON to determine whether the run was useful, safe, grounded, and ready for improvement handoff. The repo needs a deterministic evaluator artifact that summarizes live synthesis readiness without making live calls or exposing secrets.
acceptance_criteria: A deterministic evaluator can read a synthesis output JSON artifact and emit a public-safe evaluator artifact without making network calls.

Evaluator output includes provider, model, packet_id, token usage, cost estimate availability, grounding_passed, flagged_sentence_count, review reasons, governor_verdict, no_accepted_graph_writes, and final evaluator_verdict GO/PARTIAL/NO-GO.
Evaluator output includes remediation suggestions that reference repo modules and expected next-ticket scope without raw prompts, secrets, private paths, or raw source text.
Evaluator blocks or redacts unsafe/private fields using existing public/private safety helpers where available.
Unit tests cover GO, PARTIAL, and NO-GO fixture artifacts and prove no OPENAI_API_KEY is required.
non_goals: Do not make live OpenAI HTTP calls from the evaluator.
Do not replace synthesis_grounding, synthesis_review_threshold_policy, or autonomous_synthesis_governor.
Do not auto-promote evaluator suggestions into tickets/ or edit TICKET_QUEUE.md.
Do not write accepted graph rows or public-site data.
rollback_plan: Remove the evaluator module, runner script, README section, and evaluator tests. Existing synthesize --packet behavior remains unchanged because the evaluator is read-only over generated artifacts.
